stories.headline: Iran war energy shock drives nuclear power plans in Asia and Africa
stories.summary: The Iran war has exposed the fragility of global fossil fuel supply chains. Now dozens of countries across Asia and Africa are turning to nuclear power as their answer.

stories.headline: Trump Says Iran Deal Getting Closer as Lebanon Truce Starts
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump claimed Iran has made key concessions in an ongoing negotiation to end the seven-week war, while a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon increased the prospect of a broader peace.

stories.headline: Yield Bets Pay Off for Traders Willing to Tune Out War Risks

stories.headline: Trump Says Iran Deal Getting Closer; Netflix Drops On Outlook Miss | Bloomberg Brief 4/17/2026
stories.summary: US equity futures rise after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 hit fresh record highs. President Trump says a peace deal with Iran could come "fairly soon." Shares of Netflix extend decline after the streaming company's forecast fell short of analysts’ expectations and the announcement of co-founder Reed Hastings's resignation from the board. Veronica Clark of Citi anticipates a Fed rate cut in September. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Goldman Warns Continued Market Recovery Hinges on Rates Relief
stories.summary: The recent stock market rally that has driven both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 to records needs central banks to pivot back toward rate cuts to sustain its momentum, according to Christian Mueller-Glissmann, head of asset allocation research at Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

stories.headline: World Leaders Turn to China’s Xi as Iran War Drags On
stories.summary:
"The international order is crumbling into disarray," says Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

After peace talks between the US and Iran floundered, world leaders are rushing to meet with Xi, signaling growing expectations for China to be a credible force for peace. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: German Carriers Want to Tap Strategic Fuel Reserves, NATO Supply

stories.headline: HKEX Plans to Cut Trade Settlement to One Day From End of 2027
stories.summary: Hong Kong’s stock exchange operator is planning to halve the time it takes to settle stock trades, matching a global push toward shorter settlement windows despite concerns over the operational hurdles for Western investors.

stories.headline: Acting ICE Director Says He Plans to Resign in May
stories.summary: Todd Lyons said he would leave to spend more time with his family. He has spoken about a surge in threats against ICE officers, saying that he knew the reality firsthand.

stories.headline: Watch: Sunday elections — Bulgaria's fight for stable government?
